title: Dodgers notes: No White House visit? and other questions
description: As all three games in D.C. in April are day games, the Dodgers most likely won’t visit the White House then
fullText:
The Los Angeles Dodgers are back-to-back World Series champions, but they may not have back-to-back White House visits.

Bill Plunkett of the OC Register has all of the details of how a trip to 1600 Pennsylvania Ave may or may not happen.

The main roadblock is that the Dodgers are playing the Washington Nationals in D.C. the first weekend in April, which also coincides with Passover and Easter. Furthermore, the games are all day games making the visit that much more difficult.

As Bill covers in the article, they have an off day the Thursday before they start the series with the Nationals, and they have an off day between a New York and Philly road trip in July. None of those dates are without issues, however.

It remains to be seen if they go to Washington at a later date.

During the last World Baseball Classic in 2023, Team Japan had three future Dodgers playing for them – Shohei Ohtani, Yoshinobu Yamamoto, and Roki Sasaki. Dylan Hernandez of the California Post wonders if there are any other current Team Japan players that may become Dodgers in the future.

One player is Hiroto Takahashi, a 23-year-old righty who pitched in the WBC three years ago and got some people talking. He also in pitching this year but doesn’t seem to have improved nor declined on the last three years. The Dodgers are known for their pitching coaching staff, and they could help Takahashi take the next step. He is not expected to leave Japan for the States for at least two more years, and scouts currently have him as a number 3 or 4 starter.

Katie Woo published the annual Dodgers Spring Training mailbag in The Athletic. Main topics of discussion include what will Roki Sasaki’s role actually look like this season, and what will Tommy Edman’s role be when he returns.

Most interesting to me was the discussion of who Edman’s replacement will be to start the season, and who of the three of Alex Freeland, Santiago Espinal, and Hyeseong Kim will make the 26-man roster to start the season. Spoiler alert – Freeland is the odd man out. Espinal, Kim and Miguel Rojas could be on rotation to cover the second base position, just as we all thought at the beginning of Spring Training.

title: Providence vs St. John’s Prediction, Picks & Odds for Today's Big East Tournament Game
description: Providence vs. St. John's predictions and college basketball picks. The Red Storm snuff out the Friars on Thursday, March 12.
fullText:
Want to get more Covers content? Add us as a preferred source on your Google account here.

After winning the regular season conference championship outright, the No. 1 St. John’s Red Storm make their Big East Tournament debut today against the No. 9 seed Providence Friars.

Things got heated in the brawl-fest that was the February 14 meeting between these programs, setting the stage for an intense environment at Madison Square Garden.

The Red Storm will be out for blood, so I’m backing the home favorite with my Providence vs. St. John’s predictions and college basketball picks for Thursday, March 12.

Providence vs St. John’s best bet: St. John's -10.5 (-110)

The St. John’s Red Storm dominated the Big East, winning 16 of their last 17 games — going 11-6 ATS in that span — to wrap up the regular season crown.

Playing in the confines of their home arena, the Red Storm still have a job to do in the tournament. Rick Pitino won’t have his squad overlook the Providence Friars, which went 1-1 against them in the regular season and caused a full-on melee, replete with six ejections, less than a month ago.

Pitino has a storied March career, and he is 6-1 ATS in postseason games during his stint with the Red Storm.

Both meetings in the regular season resulted in an Under, and it wasn’t particularly close, with an average margin of 19 points.

St. John’s has a dominant defense, ranking third in adjusted efficiency in its last 10 games. The issue that could arise later in the postseason is on the other side of the ball, as the Red Storm rank 341st in effective field goal percentage in that span.

Star big man Zuby Ejiofor is the go-to man for offense and averages 23.5 points in the regular season against Providence.

Zuby dominates per usual while Dillon Mitchell (4.9 assists in his last seven games) continues thriving in his newfound facilitator role.

Dillon Mitchell Over 3.5 assists

Spread: Providence +11.5 (-110) | St. John’s -11.5 (-110)

Moneyline: Providence +475 | St. John’s -650

Over/Under: Over 158.5 (-110) | Under 158.5 (-110)

St. John’s is 6-1 ATS in postseason games during the Rick Pitino era. Find more college basketball betting trends for Providence vs. St. John’s.

Madison Square Garden, New York, NY

Odds are correct at the time of publishing and are subject to change.

title: Atletico Madrid Join AC Milan in the Race for Lazio Defender
description: Atletico Madrid has become the latest club to show interest in Lazio defender Mario Gila, who is also a serious target for AC Milan.The BackgroundWith a contract expiring in June 2027 and no renewal n...
fullText:
Atletico Madrid has become the latest club to show interest in Lazio defender Mario Gila, who is also a serious target for AC Milan.

With a contract expiring in June 2027 and no renewal negotiations on the horizon, all signs point towards the Spaniard’s summer exit.

Gila has been heavily linked with a move to the Rossoneri, where he would be reunited with Igli Tare, the former Lazio sporting director who brought the 25-year-old to the Italian capital in 2022.

La Gazzetta dello Sport (via La Lazio Siamo Noi) confirmed Milan’s interest in Gila, revealing that the Barcelona native was one of the names mentioned in the meeting between the club’s head coach, Max Allegri, and CEO Giorgio Furlani. Bayern Munich’s Kim Min-jae is considered the alternative option.

The San Siro outfit has some company in the race, as AS (via La Lazio Siamo Noi) reveals that Atletico Madrid are also keeping tabs on Gila. The Colchoneros could launch an attempt to sign the Spaniard should either Clement Lenglet or Jose Gimenez leave the club in the summer.

In addition to Milan and Atletico, Gila had also landed on the shortlist of several other top clubs, including Inter Milan. But despite having a host of suitors vying for his signature, Gila’s sale is unlikely to generate a major profit for Lazio due to his expiring contract and Real Madrid’s 50% sell-on fee.

The Rossoneri are reportedly hoping to buy him for €15 million. In this case, the Biancocelesti would only receive €7.5 million.

fullText:

title: Kone ruled out vs Bologna, El Aynaoui to take his place
description: Gian Piero Gasperini is also missing Manu Koné. Tonight, at 6:45 PM, Roma will face Bologna at the Dall’Ara stadium in the first leg of their Europa League round of 16 tie, but the Giallorossi coac...
fullText:
Gian Piero Gasperini is also missing Manu Koné. Tonight, at 6:45 PM, Roma will face Bologna at the Dall’Ara stadium in the first leg of their Europa League round of 16 tie, but the Giallorossi coach will be without the French midfielder.

This morning, the Giallorossi took to the field for a final training session before the match, but the number 17—as reported by Sky Sport—has suffered a muscle strain. He will be unavailable, but could be on the bench at best.

Neil El Aynaoui is ready to replace Koné.

Gasperini will rely on the Moroccan midfielder to replace the 2001-born player.

Niccolò Pisilli will complete the midfield, with Bryan Cristante acting as a striker.

The Giallorossi coach expected to have no midfielders missing, but he’ll have to address this issue as well.

Tonight, he’ll be without Gianluca Mancini, suspended, in defense, all the known attacking absences, and, last but not least, the French midfielder.

title: Jets free agency tracker: Live rumors, news, updates on signings with $73 million cap space
description: New York enters a pivotal offseason.
fullText:
Jets free agency tracker: Live rumors, news, updates on signings with $73 million cap space originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The New York Jets have a lot to do in free agency, and Aaron Glenn's head coaching job might depend on it.

They've already been active in the trade market, and there are still plenty of rumors and signings to come to try and improve a team that has plenty of needs.

The Jets made a move Monday before free agency even opened to try and address one of those spots, acquiring safety Minkah Fitzpatrick from the Miami Dolphins.

That's just a start. The Jets have more than $73 million in available cap space, according to Over The Cap.

MORE: Check out our full NFL free agency tracker here

The looming need, of course, is at quarterback. Just last offseason, the Jets signed Justin Fields, and that didn't work out. He technically enters free agent week still under contract, but there's an expectation the Jets will look to do something different.

They won't need a new RB, because they gave Breece Hall the franchise tag. But they definitely need help at wide receiver for whoever the new quarterback will be.

That's to say nothing of needs along the line and on defense. There's a lot of work for the Jets to do.

We'll be keeping track of all the happenings right here.

MORE: Steelers have a QB plan B if Aaron Rodgers retires

Refresh for the latest. Times all ET.

8 a.m.: A new potential backup QB option hit the market: The Ravens released Cooper Rush on Thursday. He was 9-5 as a fill-in starter with the Cowboys before going 0-2 as a starter with the Ravens in 2025.

6:45 a.m.: Over The Cap lists updated salary cap space for every team in the NFL -- although it's a moving target and hasn't necessarily accounted for every free agent signing or release yet. But for what it's worth, the Jets have $37 million in space as the site stands at the start of Thursday.

11:45 p.m.: As things wind down on the first day of free agency officially being open, there are still a lot of big-name free agents out there. The likes of Kyler Murray, Jauan Jennings, Stefon Diggs, Joel Bitonio, Joey Bosa, Jaquan Brisker, Dallas Goedert and more are still out there.

10:30 p.m.: There's a wild sentiment going around on social media that might be painfully true -- Geno Smith might be the Jets' best QB since Geno Smith.

4:30 p.m.: Former Jets WR John Metchie is signing a one-year contract with the Carolina Panthers, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.

3:55 p.m.: The Jets have signed an offensive lineman from the Raiders, Dylan Parham, for two years and $20 million, according to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport.

2 p.m.: The Jets have their new kicker: Cade York signs, via Brian Costello.

1:45 p.m.: Max Mitchell, an offensive tackle, has re-signed to return to the Jets, according to ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

1:30 p.m.: The Jets have lost defensive lineman Michael Clemons to a contract in free agency with the Colts, according to The Athletic's James Boyd.

1:20 p.m.: A good reminder on X from NFL analyst Kevin Massare: "Apparently another reminder needed on NFL Free Agency. It doesn't END at 4pm. It STARTS then. There's a large list of players who are currently unsigned that will greatly help good football teams this year."

11:30 a.m.: The Jets were almost certainly never going to make a contract offer to Daniel Jones as a free agent on a transition tag from the Colts. But now they can't, because Jones has signed a two-year, $100 million deal to stay in Indy.

8:40 a.m.: The Jets still need wide receiver help. Maybe they make a move toward that today.

6:45 a.m.: It's a big day in the NFL free agent process. No deals are actually official yet. At 4 p.m. ET (1 p.m. PT) on Wednesday, contracts can become official and players can fully join their new teams. That's when teams will start making lots of announcements on social media. This is also the day many releases can become official across the league, which could create the potential for more deals.

11 p.m.: One thing worth noting is that the Jets could have more moves coming soon because certain releases don't actually process until the league year begins on Wednesday. That'll impact available players and cap room.

5:20 p.m.: If any team wanted to get weird with a running back addition, Devonta Freeman wants to make an NFL comeback. But he's 34, so that would be quite the thing.

3:51 p.m.: The Jets land a new defensive back, Nashon Wright from the Chicago Bears, on a one-year, $5.5 million contract. Report came from Ian Rapoport.

3:47 p.m.: This list of QBs to play for the Jets since the last time Geno Smith did -- it's depressing.

3:30 p.m.: ESPN's Adam Schefter has reported that the Jaguars aren't interested in trading Brian Thomas Jr. If true, that takes a potential Jets target off the board.

2:15 p.m.: The Jets have found a quarterback. They traded a sixth-round pick to the Raiders for Geno Smith and a seventh-round pick, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.

10:45 a.m.: Something to note as the free agency tampering window continues into a second day: Things will definitely be slower today. Guys that weren't signed immediately usually weren't signed immediately for a reason. Either they really are taking their time intentionally, or they don't have the interest they want. Plenty more deals will come, but they could be stretched out over the next handful of days.

10:15 a.m.: This is a list of some of the top remaining free agent QBs, something the Jets likely still need to look into.

8:35 a.m.: There are a lot of reviews going around of the Jets losing Alijah Vera-Tucker, and most are pointing out that he missed 41 games in four seasons. Jets' fans may not miss him, although a departure to division rival New England hurts a bit more.

7:50 a.m.: Kyler Murray's free agency rumors have been quiet, which could matter to the Jets. 

6:45 a.m.: A couple key things to note as Tuesday gets underway. Free agency operates much the same way today as it did Monday. It's still the "legal tampering" period. Deals can't be made official until Wednesday. There are still a good chunk of free agents on the board, so more news should emerge throughout the day.

11:30 p.m.: As the week goes on, the Jets' QB situation will have to continue to take shape. But it's a solid first day for New York.

10:30 p.m.: A couple other signings to catch up with here. The Jets bring in fullback Andrew Beck, but they lose Quincy Williams to a signing with the Browns -- both reported by Jordan Schultz.

10 p.m.: One that slid under the radar earlier: The Jets also signed safety Dane Belton, per ESPN. He's another safety upgrade along with the trade for Minkah Fitzpatrick.

4:26 p.m.: After a quick flurry, it's been pretty quiet for the Jets. A decision on QB will be the big thing still to come.

4:07 p.m.: SNY is reporting that the Jaguars are shopping WR Brian Thomas Jr. in a trade, and the Jets may be interested.

2:20 p.m.: News that Mike Evans is leaving the Tampa Bay Buccaneers to sign with the San Francisco 49ers is shocking. It also officially removes the Jets from possibly ending up with Evans.

2:17 p.m.: The Jets sign DT David Onyemata to a one-year deal, per NFL Network's Mike Garafolo.

1:35 p.m.: Former Jets kicker Nick Folk is signing with the Atlanta Falcons, per NFL Network's Tom Pelissero. The Jets are also losing offensive lineman John Simpson to the Ravens.

1:09 p.m.: The Jets are busy. DeMario Davis is returning to town from the Saints. It's a two-year, $22 million deal, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.

1:01 p.m.: The Jets stick on the pass rush train with Kingsley Enagbare on a one-year deal for $10 million, per ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

1 p.m.: The Jets have signed pass rusher Joseph Ossai to a three-year, $36 million contract, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.

12:30 p.m.: A slow first half hour, with the highlights being Alec Pierce getting $116 million to return to the Colts, and Kenneth Walker signing with the Chiefs. Lots still to be done.

12 noon: The NFL's legal tampering period opens. Reports from insiders will come soon about agreed-upon signings. Nothing can be officially signed until Wednesday.

11:45 a.m.: Interesting news from elsewhere in the AFC East: It sounds like Tua Tagovailoa may follow his release from the Dolphins by signing with the Falcons.

11:10 a.m.: Would Mike Evans consider the Jets? Reports indicate he may leave Tampa Bay. The Jets might be a hard sell, though, given they're not a clear contender immediately.

10:45 a.m.: The official negotiating window opens at 12 noon on Monday, so that's when to start expecting rumors to start humming.

9:30 a.m.: The Jets began the action even before free agency, acquiring Minkah Fitzpatrick from the Miami Dolphins in exchange for the Chargers' 2026 seventh-round pick.

The Jets add Dylan Parham to the offensive line from the Raiders.

The Jets' new kicker, or at least first member of the competition, is Cade York.

The Jets have kept offensive tackle Max Mitchell, re-signing him to a new deal.

Nashon Wright joins from the Chicago Bears on a one-year contract to boost the Jets' secondary.

The Jets have a start at the QB position, trading for Geno Smith from the Raiders.

The Jets have a fullback for the season ahead, signing Andrew Beck.

Dane Belton was also signed Monday to help the secondary for the Jets.

Another defensive upgrade: David Onyemata to play defensive tackle on a one-year deal.

The defense keeps getting better. Old friend DeMario Davis is back on a two-year contract.

Another pass rush upgrade: Kingsley Enagbare on a one-year contract.

The Jets have upgraded their pass rush by signing Joseph Ossai to a three-year deal.

While not a signing, the Minkah Fitzpatrick trade was a perfect tone-setting move to begin Monday before the negotiating window even opened.

Last week, the Jets gave Breece Hall the franchise tag, securing their RB1 at least for this season.

Offensive lineman John Simpson has departed for the Ravens.

Former Jets kicker Nick Folk has signed with the Falcons.

Star linebacker Quincy Williams has departed for the Cleveland Browns.

Michael Clemons, a defensive lineman, has left for the Colts.

John Metchie is leaving the Jets' weak WR group and joining the Panthers.

Cowboys shunned by Eagles' free agent for AFC West

Panthers shell out $165 million for 2 free agent stars

This is why the Cowboys failed in trading for Maxx Crosby

Ravens' former 1st-round pick retires after 8 NFL seasons

Jets trade rumor would mean major WR upgrade

Ravens have a $38 million last-gasp Lamar Jackson solution to contract problem

title: Real Madrid’s Santiago Bernabeu set to host ‘Finalissima’ between Spain and Argentina – report
description: Real Madrid’s iconic Santiago Bernabeu Stadium has surfaced as the top contender to host the highly anticipated 2026 ‘Finalissima’ between European champions Spain and Copa America victors...
fullText:
Real Madrid’s iconic Santiago Bernabeu Stadium has surfaced as the top contender to host the highly anticipated 2026 ‘Finalissima’ between European champions Spain and Copa America victors Argentina, reports Arancha Rodriguez of COPE.

While an official confirmation from UEFA and CONMEBOL is still pending, the match, scheduled to take place on Friday, March 27, 2026, is now expected to take place at Real Madrid’s home stadium.

The showpiece event was originally slated for the Lusail Stadium in Qatar, the site of Argentina’s 2022 World Cup triumph.

However, that escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have forced organisers to seek alternative venues for the safety of players and fans.

Set to host the ‘Finalissima 2026’ (Photo by Denis Doyle/Getty Images)

While UEFA previously maintained that Qatar remained the primary host, the search for a European “Plan B” has intensified in recent days, with Real Madrid’s renovated Bernabéu now leading the pack.

Choosing the Bernabeu is not without its hurdles. For starters, a scheduling conflict exists at the nearby Riyadh Air Metropolitano, which is set to host a friendly between Morocco and Ecuador on the same evening, raising potential security concerns.

Other venues, such as the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, US,and Prince Moulay Abdellah Stadium in Rabat, Morocco, as well as Wembley Stadium in London, UK had been in consideration.

But, Real Madrid’s Santiago Bernabeu has now become the leading candidate to host what promises to be a historic clash potentially featuring the first-ever competitive meeting between Barcelona legend Lionel Messi and rising superstar Lamine Yamal.

title: Ex-Barcelona midfielder reflects on Ansu Fati’s brilliance and lessons at the club: ‘He will be back on form’
description: Former Barcelona midfielder Ludovit Reis may not be among the most famous names to have passed through the club in recent years, but his time in Catalonia gave him the chance to witness the rise of se...
fullText:
Former Barcelona midfielder Ludovit Reis may not be among the most famous names to have passed through the club in recent years, but his time in Catalonia gave him the chance to witness the rise of several talented players.

The Dutch midfielder spent a season with Barcelona’s reserve side, Barça Atlètic, where he shared the dressing room with a number of young prospects who would later make their mark at the top level.

Among them were Ronald Araujo, Nico Gonzalez, Ilaix Moriba, and Ansu Fati.

Looking back on that period, Reis spoke warmly about the friendships he formed with those players and revealed that they still stay in touch today.

“I played in the reserve team with [Ronald] Araujo, Nico Gonzalez, Ilaix, and Ansu. We still comment on each other’s Instagram posts and things like that.

“We don’t call each other every day, but we still have a good connection. Back then, before his injuries, Ansu Fati was also incredibly good.

“He was extraordinary. Unfortunately, things are more difficult for him after all his injuries. But I have every confidence that he will be back on form at Monaco,” he told VoetbalPrimeur, as quoted by SPORT.

Reis also spoke about the moment he joined Barcelona as a young player, describing the experience as one of the most remarkable chapters of his career.

Moving from the Netherlands to Spain at an early stage of his development exposed him to a completely different football culture and environment.

“It was perhaps the most extraordinary adventure I’ve had in my football career, moving from the far north to the warmth of Barcelona. As a young man, I learned and saw a lot.

“Barcelona is undoubtedly one of the biggest clubs in the world. For me, taking that step was simply a wonderful learning process.”

Reis spoke very highly of Ansu Fati. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

During his time at the club, Reis also had the opportunity to train with the first team on several occasions. Those sessions allowed him to observe some of Barcelona’s biggest stars up close.

One player who particularly impressed him was Sergio Busquets, whose intelligence on the pitch stood out immediately.

The Dutch midfielder explained how different it felt to watch the legendary midfielder operate in training.

“What else can be said about Busquets? Everyone knows Busquets’ qualities.

“When you see him in person, playing with him in training and playing positional games, you understand why people talk about him like that.

“Messi, obviously, is in a different league. But I didn’t think Busquets was normal.”

Reis also mentioned his relationship with fellow Dutch international Frenkie de Jong, who was already part of Barcelona’s first team during that period.

Although the two players were usually involved with different squads, they still found opportunities to speak and share experiences.

“Frenkie was more with the first team, and I was with the reserves. We also had a busy schedule. He was also in Barcelona with his girlfriend. We talked, of course.

“When I was in the first team, we talked constantly. Everything was going well with him. He’s a great person.”

title: Commanders 65-man offseason depth chart – updated of the first day of the 2026 league year
description: Washington’s GM Adam Peters is still working hard to fill out the roster with quality free agents
fullText:
There have been several changes to the Commanders roster since we published the last depth chart update yesterday. Here’s a quick summary of the reported transactions that took place over the past week or so:

QB Marcus Mariota – 1 year ($7m)

LT Laremy Tunsil – 2 years ($30.2m)

These 8 players are highlighted in pink on the depth chart.

CB Amik Robertson – 2 yrs, $16m

EDGE K’Lavon Chaisson – 1 yr, $12m

TE Chig Okonkwo – 3 yrs, up to $30m

S Nick Cross – 2 yrs, up to $14m

DE Charles Omenihu – 1 yr, up to $7m

I have taken the approach on defense of listing 5 defensive line positions, 5 linebacker spots, and 5 defensive back positions to allow you to configure 3-man, 4-man and 5-man fronts in your own mind. There’s some guesswork involved here, and some players (think Daron Payne) will line up in multiple positions, so I have just tried to make the defensive side of the ball make as much sense as I can while the team is in transition to an unclear scheme under Daronte Jones.

The Commanders currently have 36 defensive players, 26 offensive players and 3 special teams players.

Please note that assigned positions; they do not necessarily represent the thinking of Washington’s coaches or front office, nor are they necessarily consistent with fan consensus. This chart represents my personal interpretation, and may not reflect the thoughts of other writers on Hogs Haven. Finally, when it comes to backup players, I don’t put much effort into making sure that they are on the right or left or behind the specific player that they backup. I mostly just try to fit everyone on the chart efficiently.

As mentioned above, for the moment, the defensive layout is a bit schizophrenic. We should get more clarity as we move through the offseason and training camp.

Free agency is not over by any means. The Commanders still need to add another two dozen players to the offseason roster, and they currently hold only 6 picks in April’s draft.

That means that Adam Peters is still looking to add another 18-20 veteran and college free agents over the coming weeks.

With 65 players, the Commanders haven’t quite reached the number of players that will comprise the 53-man roster and 17-man practice squad in 2025. There are still thin spots on the roster to patch up ahead of the draft.

The team needs to add more ‘impact’ players in free agency — with many fans focused on WR, RB, and CB — but the 2nd wave of free agency will offer opportunities to get some lesser known or less costly players into the fold ahead of the draft. The Maxx Crosby situation may also add a bit of turmoil as teams try to figure out how the free agency dynamic is changed by the many ripple effects of the Ravens reversal.

title: ‘The legacy will be written here and now’: Keith Thurman eager to become champion once again
description: Keith Thurman says he still has enough left in the tank to demonstrate the best of himself.
fullText:
We’re now only a couple of weeks away from the March 28 super welterweight title defense between reigning titleholder Sebastian Fundora defending against challenger Keith Thurman. Both fighters have just appeared for a virtual press conference and to answer media questions, and here is some of what both had to say with the full video in the link at the top.

“OGs aren’t really in the sport no more, you know. So once you get old in this sport you shouldn’t be boxing no more, that’s what I think. but I fought a lot of fighters. I just see Keith Thurman as another one.”

“I don’t pick the fights. These guys, the promoters and PBC, they pick them for me. It is what it is. He has two belts right now, I have a belt, we can still unify. It never happened but that fight can still happen so I’m not worried about it.”

“I think my skills are better, that’s why we’re fighting him. I’m the champion, I’m defending my title, and I plan on keeping my title come March 28.”

“He’s going to see. He’s going to bring it. He needs to do what he always has done…He’s confident and he feels like he’s growing…He’s going to see what an OG can do and the name is ‘One Time’ for a reason, not a short season. March 28, he’s going to find out.”

“It’s definitely the second leg of the race…This is where the legacy is written or not. The legacy will be written here and now or it will be something that was. But great champions, they become champions again. And you can’t stop a true champion from rising to the top…I’ve been here before and I’m here to make a statement and do as much great things as I can until we hang these gloves up. But we’ve got a few more years…”

title: Iowa State vs Texas Tech Prediction, Picks & Odds for Today's Big 12 Tournament Game
description: Our college basketball predictions, picks, and odds for Iowa State vs. Texas Tech in the Big 12 Conference Tournament on Thursday, March 12.
fullText:
Want to get more Covers content? Add us as a preferred source on your Google account here.

The No. 4 seed Texas Tech Red Raiders face the No. 5 Iowa State Cyclones in the quarterfinals of the Big 12 Conference Tournament this afternoon.

The Cyclones demolished Arizona State 91-42 yesterday, and my Iowa State vs. Texas Tech predictions expect them to build on that momentum. Here are my college basketball picks for this clash on March 11.

Iowa State vs Texas Tech best bet: Iowa State -5.5 (-110)

The Iowa State Cyclones are in a revenge spot against the Texas Tech Red Raiders, who enter on a two-game skid. Texas Tech was the only team to beat the Cyclones at home this season, upsetting them 82-73 two weeks ago.

The Raiders went 14-for-29 from deep in that contest and have been extremely perimeter-oriented since All-American forward JT Toppin tore his ACL. With Iowa State third in conference play in 3-point defense (32.8%), expect major regression in that area.

Texas Tech has struggled with turnovers and rebounding lately, and ISU will turn those extra scoring chances into points.

Cyclones point guard Tamin Lipsey has dropped 4+ dimes in 24 of 28 games — including the previous clash with Texas Tech.

title: BYD linked with Aston Martin buyout as Lawrence Stroll edges closer to ‘breaking point’
description: BYD are reportedly considering buying an existing Formula 1 team instead of launching a new outfit from scratch, and Aston Martin have been mentioned as a possible target. Chinese automaker BYD could be preparing to submit a bid to join Formula 1.
fullText:
BYD are reportedly considering buying an existing Formula 1 team instead of launching a new outfit from scratch, and Aston Martin have been mentioned as a possible target.

Chinese automaker BYD could be preparing to submit a bid to join Formula 1. The league recently grew to 11 teams with Cadillac’s addition, but Liberty Media have often spoken about wanting an even dozen.

Audi chose to buy Sauber ahead of the 2026 season and will compete using their own power units. BYD haven’t decided which route they’ll take, but both options are under consideration.

A recent report suggested that Lawrence Stroll’s interest in Formula 1 may be waning, with Aston Martin struggling at the back of the grid. That’s led to speculation within the paddock that a sale could be on the cards.

Stroll has already spent six times more than he originally planned to try and turn Aston into a front-runner. Instead, they’ve started F1’s new era sitting 10th in the standings, only ahead of Cadillac, and have pointed fingers at engine supplier Honda.

As reported by Auto Motor und Sport, Stroll “could soon reach his breaking point,” which would make Aston Martin an appealing acquisition for BYD.

From Honda’s perspective, there is currently no desire to pull out of F1 despite their earlier plans to leave before the 2026 regulations come into effect.

In terms of value, Aston Martin were estimated at around $3 billion (£2.2 billion) last year, ranking them just behind F1’s established ‘big four’ teams.

Rumours that Alpine could be put up for sale have been a constant undercurrent in the paddock, even as majority owners Renault continue to insist otherwise.

The switch to Mercedes power units has fuelled speculation that Alpine might appeal to BYD as a logical entry point. It gives the team a stronger technical foundation without the need for immediate overhauls.

Renault CEO Francois Provost, who isn’t known for his passion for motorsport, has already started pulling the manufacturer out of other racing commitments like the World Endurance Championship. Still, they’re not actively looking to offload their 76% share in the F1 team.

Meanwhile, Otro Capital, which holds the remaining 24%, has reportedly opened discussions with potential buyers. There’s talk that Toto Wolff and Mercedes might get involved, and Christian Horner’s name has also come up in connection with a possible stake.

Gary Anderson says Adrian Newey made a Honda claim in Australia that won’t have gone down well

Mercedes look to block Red Bull and Ferrari from upgrading 2026 engines using FIA rule

BYD could become China’s first-ever F1 team as they explore a ‘competitive involvement’

title: How to watch Wisconsin’s first Big Ten Tournament game on Thursday
description: The Badgers are back in action on Thursday.
fullText:
The Wisconsin Badgers are kicking off the postseason with their first game in the Big Ten Tournament on Thursday. As the No. 5 seed, they’ll face the No. 12 seed Washington Huskies, who beat the No. 13 seed USC Trojans on Wednesday in the second round of the Big Ten Tournament.

Wisconsin and Washington faced off at the end of February, with the Badgers getting off to a hot start and blowing the Huskies out in Spokane 90-73. In that game, Braeden Carrington was the storyline for the Badgers, scoring a career-high 32 points and a program-high nine threes in the win. Nick Boyd also had 22 points, while Wisconsin hit 17 threes as a whole.

In Washington’s overtime win over USC on Wednesday, the Huskies ran only a seven-man rotation and saw four players total at least 39 minutes. So, fatigue could play a factor on Thursday, hence the need for another strong start from the Badgers.

Should Wisconsin win, they’d face the Illinois Fighting Illini in the Big Ten Quarterfinals. Illinois picked up the No. 4 seed in the tournament with a win over the Maryland Terrapins on Sunday, placing the Badgers in the fifth seed. The two teams also faced off in January, with the Badgers having an impressive 92-90 overtime victory on the road.

For Thursday, Nolan Winter’s status remains in the air, although it seems likelier that he’ll sit once again for the Badgers after an ankle injury popped up last week.

Here’s how you can watch Thursday’s game for the Badgers against Washington.

title: Ravens agree to terms with OL Jovaughn Gwyn
description: The Ravens are making an addition to their offensive line.
fullText:
The Ravens are making an addition to their offensive line.

According to multiple reports, they have agreed to terms with Jovaughn Gwyn. It is a one-year deal for the former Falcon in Baltimore.

Gwyn only played in two games during the 2023 and 2024 seasons, but appeared in all 17 games for the Falcons during the 2025 season. He played 11 offensive snaps and 73 special teams snaps in those appearances.

The move to Baltimore will allow Gwyn to keep working with offensive line coach Dwayne Ledford, who joined the Ravens' staff after spending the last five years coaching the offensive line in Atlanta.

title: Lady Vol named SEC Golf Freshman of the Week
description: Lady Vol named SEC Golf Freshman of the Week.
fullText:
Tennessee's Madison Messimer was named SEC Women's Golf Freshman of the Week when the league announced its weekly awards on Wednesday.

Messimer won the first individual tournament title of her college career this week at the Chris Banister Classic. The South Carolina native finished first in a field of 93 golfers as she finished the event with a three-round score of 2-under 214, leading the No. 19 Lady Vols to the team championship at the event. Tennessee finished the tournament with a team score of 5-over par, finishing 35 strokes ahead of runner-up Chattanooga. Jacksonville State finished in third place (+42) and Longwood came in fourth (+49).

Messimer closed out the tournament with a 4-under 68 after posting a score of 73 in each of the first two rounds. Her third-round score is the best round score is her best in relation to par this season. She was the only golfer to post an under par score in the final round, which was contested Tuesday at Trail Golf Course at Silver Lake in Gadsden. Alabama.

Messimer was one of four Lady Vols to finish inside the top-10 of the individual standings. Kyra Van Kan posted an individual runner-up finish with a score of 216. Kaopin Thapasit (+5) finished fifth and Sofie Engeseth finished tied for seventh with a score of 223.

Follow Vols Wire on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).

title: Former Leicester City midfielder set to leave Antwerp on a free transfer this summer
description: As reported by David Van den Broeck, former Leicester City midfielder Dennis Praet will leave Antwerp this summer. Praet’s contract is up in the summer but Antwerp won’t negotiate a new deal with ...
fullText:
As reported by David Van den Broeck, former Leicester City midfielder Dennis Praet will leave Antwerp this summer. Praet’s contract is up in the summer but Antwerp won’t negotiate a new deal with the midfielder due to wage demands. Antwerp are looking to cut costs, and with Praet being one of the bigger earners in the squad, it is understandable that they will have to part ways. On top of that, his impact on the side has not been enough for them to justify the spending required to keep him.

Praet joined Antwerp from Leicester City in 2024 on a free transfer. It was Praet’s first return to Belgium, having broken through at Anderlecht and left to join Sampdoria in 2016. His performances in Italy earned him a move to England to join Leicester City, where he would make 60 appearances between 2019 and 2021. However, during the 2021/2022 season Praet was loaned out by Leicester City, with the midfielder returning to Italy to join Torino. On is return from the loan spell in Serie A, Praet played a further two seasons with Leicester City before leaving.

Kiki Kouyaté and Glenn Bijl are also set to leave at the end of their contracts in the summer. Vincent Janssen, one of the other top earners at the club, may yet renew his deal on lower wages.

title: Report – Inter Milan Open To Selling German Defender For €40M Despite Imminent Contract Extension
description: Inter Milan could reportedly offload star defender Yann Bisseck in the summer despite ongoing discussions over a contract renewal.According to Gazzetta dello Sport via FCInter1908, the Nerazzurri coul...
fullText:
Inter Milan could reportedly offload star defender Yann Bisseck in the summer despite ongoing discussions over a contract renewal.

According to Gazzetta dello Sport via FCInter1908, the Nerazzurri could sell the German center-back at the end of the season.

Yann Bisseck joined Inter from Danish outfit Aarhus in the summer of 2023 for a reported fee of €7 million.

Furthermore, the 25-year-old has cemented his first-team status this season.

Despite starting the campaign as a backup option, the highly rated defender has convinced Cristian Chivu to hand him a regular starting berth over the course of the season.

Indeed, he has made 17 league appearances this term, starting ten of Inter’s last 12 matches in Serie A.

Bisseck’s current contract at San Siro runs until 2029, which puts the club in a strong bargaining position ahead of the summer transfer window.

Furthermore, the Serie A pacesetters are keen to tie the former Aarhus man down to a new deal in the coming months.

However, there’s still a chance for Bisseck to leave the club at the end of the season.

Indeed, the Nerazzurri would reportedly consider offers in the region of €40 million, with several European top clubs interested in the German ace.

title: Carlisle CEO Clibbens leaves after decade at club
description: Carlisle United chief executive officer Nigel Clibbens leaves his role with immediate effect after a decade at the club.
fullText:
Carlisle United chief executive officer Nigel Clibbens has left the club with immediate effect.

Clibbens joined the club in 2016, having previously held a similar role at Huddersfield Town.

But in a statement, the Cumbrians said "this was the right time to make a leadership change as the club moves into its next phase".

The club added that an interim leadership structure is in place to "ensure continuity" while focus remains on "supporting the team".

Carlisle are third in the National League with nine games to go after a 2-0 midweek win at Aldershot.

But with a 13-point gap to leaders Rochdale, the play-offs looks like their only route for securing promotion back to League Two.

title: Predicting Oregon Ducks' defensive depth chart ahead of spring season
description: An early prediction of what the Oregon Ducks' defensive depth chart will look like ahead of the 2026 spring football season.
fullText:
It's been a long start to the offseason for Oregon Duck fans, many of whom are excited to enter a new year, one that brings high expectations for another run at the national championship in Eugene.

With Dante Moore and Evan Stewart leading a talented offense that returns a long list of starters, and Matayo Uiagalelei, A'Mauri Washington, and Teitum Tuioti doing the same thing on defense, there's a lot of promise with Dan Lanning's team going into the 2026 season.

Thankfully, the wait to see some of that promise show itself is over. The spring season kicks off for the Ducks on Thursday, with the first of 15 spring practices taking place at the Hatfield-Dowlin Complex. Over the next month, we will see Oregon go through its spring workouts, leading up to the annual Spring Game on April 25.

What we see during this time won't set things in stone for the season once fall comes, but it will be a great preview for what we expect to see once games are being played in September and beyond. So what will that roster look like, and who can we expect to be the impact players down the stretch? It may be way too early to be making any of these predictions, but that's not going to stop us.

Here's our look at what the Oregon defensive depth chart looks like going into the 2026 spring season.

Other Names to Know: Jerome Simmons / Aydin Breland

Analysis: Bear Alexander returns for another year and will slot in as the starter once again. Behind him, the depth gets a little bit interesting and hard to forecast this early on. Some early names to look at, though, are Jerome Simmons, Matt Johnson, and Aydin Breland, who can likely slot in across the defensive front.

Other Names to Know: Matt Johnson / Aydin Breland / Tony Cumberland

Analysis: A'Mauri Washington, a projected first-round draft pick in 2025, is back for another year, which is massive for the Ducks. Much like the nose tackle spot, though, things get a bit unclear behind him. With the mass of transfers out at this position, it's unclear who will be his backup. I'm looking at North Carolina transfer D'Antre Robinson, Aydin Breland, Matt Johnson, and Tony Cumberland early on, though.

Other Names to Know: Tank Jones / Prince Tavizon

Analysis: Matayo Uiagalelei returns for his senior season, which again is a huge win for the Ducks. But, just like on the interior defensive line, there are some questions behind him. The edges are in better shape than the inside, depth-wise, with guys like Elijah Rushing and Tank Jones projected to have an impact, but it will be interesting to see how this depth chart fleshes out in the spring and summer.

Other Names to Know: Tank Jones

Analysis: Once again, Teitum Tuioti returns and provides some massive stability for the Ducks. Behind him, it's not hard to project that Nasir Wyatt is going to step up and have a bigger role, and Tank Jones can likely play in this spot as well.

Other Names to Know: Dutch Horisk

Analysis: Nasir Wyatt looks primed for a bigger role in this defense, assuming he can bulk up a bit this offseason, so I think he will slot in as the starter here, especially with Blake Purchase gone. I also want to see Tank Jones step up into this position as well.

Other Names to Know: Gavin Nix / Dylan Williams

Analysis: There are some questions about the linebacker spots this offseason, with Bryce Boettcher graduating. I expect someone like Devon Jackson to step up and be the guy in one of these spots. The Ducks also have a lot of young and talented players ready to have an impact.

Other Names to Know: Brayden Platt

Analysis: After stepping into a starting role this past season, Jerry Mixon projects to be one of the leaders of this defense next year, and potentially the green-dot linebacker that Oregon has been needing over the past couple of years. I am also curious to see what Gavin Nix can do with another year of growth under his belt.

Other Names to Know: Dorian Brew/Davon Benjamin

Analysis: Ify Obidegwu had a great year in 2025 as one of the starting cornerbacks, and that shouldn't change next year either. He will undoubtedly be pushed by guys like Na'eem Offord, Doran Brew, and Aaron Scott Jr., among others, but that's not a bad thing for the Ducks. The more competition, the better.

Other Names to Know: Davon Benjamin / Dorian Brew

Analysis: Brandon Finney was the star of the show this year on Oregon's defense, dominating as a true freshman and earning true freshman All-American honors. He should once again be among the best cornerbacks in the nation next year and lead the Ducks' secondary. Again, I'm curious to watch guys like Scott, Brew, and Benjamin, as well as Na'eem Offord, who may play out wide or in the nickel.

Other Names to Know: Jett Washington / Xavier Lherisse

Analysis: After earning the starting spot this past year alongside Dillon Thieneman, Flowers projects to be one of the best players on Oregon's defense once again next season. He's going to be pushed by former 5-star Trey McNutt, who was injured for much of 2025, but the Ducks' secondary looks to be very strong once again.

Other Names to Know: Trey McNutt / Devin Jackson

Analysis: With Dillon Thieneman going to the NFL draft, Oregon went into the portal and got a great replacement in Koi Perich, from Minnesota. Perich is a rangy safety who will excel in coverage, leading the secondary much like DT did last year. Behind him, I once again expect guys like Peyton Woodyard and Trey McNutt to step up.

Other Names to Know: Davon Benjamin / Aaron Scott Jr.

Analysis: Oregon went into the portal to land Baylor's Carl Williams IV, who likely will slot into the Nickel position to replace Jadon Canady, who was great for the Ducks this past year. I am also intrigued by Na'eem Offord, who could play in the nickel or out wide, as well as true freshman Davon Benjamin.

This article originally appeared on Ducks Wire: Oregon Ducks' defensive depth chart projection ahead of spring season

title: Jauan Jennings free agency rumors leave NFL analyst baffled about 49ers WR
description: JJ has to keep working to prove he's worth it.
fullText:
Jauan Jennings free agency rumors leave NFL analyst baffled about 49ers WR originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Jauan Jennings continues to have to fight for what he's worth.

The NFL is a wide receiver league at this point, yet the free agent from the San Francisco 49ers has found contract negotiations to be difficult lately.

Jennings still hasn't signed even though a bunch of the other best WRs available are now off the market, and it's confusing to some, including NFL analyst Jeff Bell.

"In a league desperate for WRs, Jauan Jennings' value in free agency has been baffling two out of the last three offseasons."

It's certainly a fair take. Jennings spent last offseason wanting a raise or a trade from the 49ers, and in the end, he still wound up on the open market this offseason rather than with San Francisco for the long run.

MORE: Why the Raiders are no longer trading Maxx Crosby to the Ravens

The advanced metrics favor what Jennings does, and so does an anecdotal view of his career, when you consider that basically any role the 49ers have needed him to do, he's performed admirably.

Because of his varied usage through his tenure in San Francisco, maybe it's hard for a team to envision Jennings in one defined role with them.

Another holdup here could be A.J. Brown on the trade market. Maybe a team that wants Brown and doesn't get him would instead go after Jennings.

There's also a talented bunch of wideouts in the 2026 NFL Draft class to choose from.

Still, it seems like Jennings could be a boost to a bunch of teams. He's overdue to be signed, and potentially for a solid payday, even if this delay signals he may not get exactly the money he's looking for.

title: Browns former star WR reveals truth about the Deshaun Watson signing
description: Former Cleveland Browns fan favorite WR Jarvis Landry was so close to landing in Atlanta with Deshaun Watson, per himself.
fullText:
It only took four seasons, but in just a short time, Jarvis Landry became a fan favorite as a Cleveland Browns wide receiver. He came to the worst team in the NFL and was a major part of the cultural turnaround that took place in Northeast Ohio.

While Landry's time in Cleveland came to a close, his story in relation to the Browns didn't end quite there.

On Wednesday, Landry announced that he and his old LSU teammate Leonard Fournette released a new podcast together called "4th and South," and one of their initial storylines was the unveiling of the truth behind the Deshaun Watson signing in 2022.

As their version of the story goes, Watson was all but signed to the Atlanta Falcons, and going with him were both Landry and Fournette.

Landry was so convinced that Watson was going to be a Falcon that he "had (his) bags packed," as he went to bed on that Thursday night. He was so deep in it that he had spent the three or four days before on the phone with the Falcons' head coach, Arthur Smith, talking about different ways that he was going to be utilized in Atlanta.

Landry woke up on that Friday morning with the car on the way to take him to his flight to Atlanta when breaking news came across the ticker, and Deshaun Watson signed with the Cleveland Browns. Both Landry and Fournette were caught totally off guard and by surprise as that decision changed the course of history in the NFL.

Landry went on to play for the New Orleans Saints, while Fournette went and signed one final contract in Buffalo.

The butterfly effect that decision had on the Cleveland Browns franchise and the NFL at large cannot be understated. The Browns had already moved on from Baker Mayfield at the time, but they would have retained three draft picks and $230M dollars over the course of 5 seasons. That's before all of the off-field baggage that Watson brought along with him.

The Browns were big game hunting at that time to find their quarterback, and who knows what that would have led to. But what we do know is that the Browns were so close to avoiding the worst transaction in NFL History.

Instead, Jimmy Haslam decided not to take "no" for an answer. He dropped the biggest bag in NFL history along with three first-round draft picks, and well, the rest is history.

This article originally appeared on Browns Wire: Browns former star WR reveals truth about the Deshaun Watson signing

title: Wisconsin hockey falls in first round of B1G men's hockey tournament
description: Things didn't go according to plan as the No. 10/11 Wisconsin Badgers men's hockey team was defeated by Ohio State in the B1G Tournament, 7-1.
fullText:
Things didn't go according to plan at the Kohl Center as the No. 10/11 Wisconsin Badgers men's hockey team was defeated by Ohio State in the Big Ten Tournament, 7-1.

Wisconsin entered the conference tournament as the No. 4 seed but failed to take care of business when hosting the No. 5 seed. OSU got on the board just five minutes into the game and that proved to be too much for Wisconsin to overcome.

The Buckeyes led 7-0 before the Badgers found the back of the net. Oddly enough, it was a short-handed goal for Freshman forward Finn Brink. Either way, that was far too little too late. Wisconsin, which was coming off a sweep over No. 6 Penn State, failed to do any damage in the Big Ten Tournament.

The seven goals they conceded were their most allowed since January 31 when they fell to Minnesota, 8-4. It's the fourth time this year the 21-12-2 Badgers have allowed seven or more goals.

Final pic.twitter.com/0kt65dO1Xd

— Wisconsin Hockey (@BadgerMHockey) March 12, 2026

Wisconsin is currently 12th in the NCAA Percentage Index, which is all that matters as the selection show approaches on Sunday, March 22. A total of 16 teams make it into the tournament, but obviously, the Badgers will need an at-large bid after their quest to win the B1G came crashing down far too soon.

title: Finalissima 2026: Lamine Yamal vs Lionel Messi set to take place at the Santiago Bernabeu
description: Football fans around the world have been eagerly waiting for one of the most exciting international clashes in recent years – Lamine Yamal against Lionel Messi.The meeting between Spain and Argentin...
fullText:
Football fans around the world have been eagerly waiting for one of the most exciting international clashes in recent years – Lamine Yamal against Lionel Messi.

The meeting between Spain and Argentina in the Finalissima has generated enormous interest since both teams secured their continental titles.

Spain earned their place in the intercontinental clash after winning UEFA Euro 2024, defeating England 2-1 in the final.

Meanwhile, Argentina confirmed their participation after lifting the Copa América trophy by beating Colombia 1-0 in extra time.

With those results, the football world immediately began dreaming about a symbolic clash between two generations: the legendary Messi and the rising Barcelona star Yamal.

Initially, organisers confirmed that the Finalissima would be staged at Lusail Stadium in Qatar.

The stadium had already hosted major matches during the 2022 FIFA World Cup, making it a logical venue for another global football event.

The match was scheduled for March 27, and preparations were already underway to host the highly anticipated encounter in Doha.

However, global events soon forced organisers to reconsider those plans.

Santiago Bernabeu could host the Finalissima. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

The ongoing conflict in the Middle East made it increasingly difficult to stage the match safely in Qatar. As a result, the Royal Spanish Football Federation began exploring alternative options.

Reports later confirmed that the match would likely move away from Doha due to the unstable situation in the region.

According to information shared by Cadena COPE, the Finalissima will now be played in Europe instead.

Several stadiums across Europe were considered for the event, but many were unavailable because of existing international fixtures scheduled during that same weekend.

After discussions between the parties involved, Real Madrid’s Santiago Bernabeu emerged as the most suitable venue.

Initially, Argentina were hesitant about playing the match in Spain, as it would give the Spanish side home advantage. Nevertheless, logistical difficulties left few viable alternatives.

With the Bernabeu now expected to host the match, the Finalissima could take place in front of a packed crowd in one of football’s most famous stadiums.

If everything goes as planned, fans may soon witness a historic night where Yamal and Messi share the same stage in Madrid.

title: Browns NFL free agency: Familiar face announces return for 2026 season
description: Cleveland Browns punter Corey Bojorquez announces his return in 2026, re-upping his contract in Cleveland for at least another year
fullText:
One of the main cogs in the wheel that has been the Cleveland Browns over the last four years has been the punter, Corey Bojorquez.

Bojorquez joined the Browns in 2022 and has been the left leg that fans have become (way too) used to over that time span. He posted late Wednesday night on Instagram that he is returning to the Dawg Pound in 2026.

Bojorquez and the special teams as a whole had an abysmal season last year. He set his career high in punts with 91 while posting his worst punt yard per kick average since his second season in 2019. What was worse was his 37-yard net average, which was the worst mark of his career. He added career highs in touchbacks and blocked kicks in a year that everyone wants to forget from a special teams perspective.

Having a good punter can be a very important weapon for a team. If you don't believe me, look no further than the Super Bowl when the Seattle special teams won the football game. Bojorquez has that type of talent, but it just eluded him in the 2025 season.

With the deal in place to bring Bojorquez back, Cleveland returns their special teams trio in its entirety from last year. Bojorquez, kicker Andre Szmyt, and long snapper Rex Sunahara will all be returning to provide that level of continuity and familiarity that is so important to this position group.

It appears punter Corey Bojorquez is back in Cleveland for another season (via Instagram) pic.twitter.com/PYlg11xfTa

— Daniel Oyefusi (@DanielOyefusi) March 12, 2026

The lone change in that position group is at the head of the snake. Byron Storer took over the duties that were left behind by Bubba Ventrone after a season full of struggles.

Storer has spent his young career cutting his teeth under Rich Bassacia in Green Bay as the special teams assistant. Storer comes to Cleveland with very high expectations for this position group.

Cleveland's new head coach, Todd Monken, is going to be a force of excellence out of all of his units. He demands accountability, and the guys he hired to coach his football team are going to be under daily pressure from the head man.

That goes triple for the special teams group, and with the return of Bojorquez, Cleveland will go into the 2026 season with the incumbents in place and ready to roll into another football season.

This article originally appeared on Browns Wire: Browns NFL free agency: Corey Bojorquez announces return in 2026

title: “Most underrated on the planet”: Alexander-Arnold lauds flawless Valverde after historic hat-trick vs Manchester City
description: Trent Alexander-Arnold has said there are no flaws in the game of Real Madrid teammate Federico Valverde.Valverde scored a first-half hat-trick as Real Madrid beatManchester City 3-0 in the first leg ...
fullText:
Trent Alexander-Arnold has said there are no flaws in the game of Real Madrid teammate Federico Valverde.

Valverde scored a first-half hat-trick as Real Madrid beatManchester City 3-0 in the first leg of their Champions League last-16 tie on Wednesday night.

It was just the second time a player had scored aChampions League hat-trick against a Premier League club before half-time. The only previous player to achieve the feat was Lionel Messi, who did so against Arsenal in 2010.

The treble was also the first of Valverde's career but Alexander-Arnold is in no doubt that the Uruguayan has long been one of the world's best.

Discussing the 27-year-old, he said Valverde belongs among the 'top tier of midfielders on the planet' and believes he does not receive the recognition he deserves.

"I sang his praises a few weeks ago but, again, I'm running out of words to describe him," Alexander-Arnold toldCBS Sports.

"He's the most underrated footballer on the planet and has been for years. Even when I wasn't here, I've admired his game so much because there's no flaw in it. There's nothing he can't do.

"The energy and what he brings to the team is absolutely immense. He never moans, he gets on with the game and he pushes himself to the limit, game in, game out. It's an incredible trait to have.

"Whether people agree or not, he's definitely in the top tier of midfielders on the planet, and has been for years."

Real Madrid head coach Alvaro Arbeloa was similarly full of praise for Valverde's performance. He said the vice-captain epitomises 'everything a Real Madrid player should be'.

"It doesn’t matter where you put him, I almost get tired of talking to him," Arbeloa told Movistar+.

"He’s the Juanito of the 21st century. Everything a Real Madrid player should be is Valverde; he deserves a night like this."

title: Kentucky vs Missouri Prediction, Picks & Odds for Today's SEC Tournament Game
description: These Kentucky vs. Missouri predictions do not so much doubt the Wildcats as they do expect a slow game on Thursday, March 12, in the SEC Tournament.
fullText:
Want to get more Covers content? Add us as a preferred source on your Google account here.

The Kentucky Wildcats overcame a sloppy first half to win in the SEC Tournament’s first round, and now they are favored against the Missouri Tigers to reach Friday.

My Kentucky vs. Missouri predictions and college basketball picks expect the Tigers to slow the game and thus slow the best Wildcat on Thursday, March 12.

Kentucky vs Missouri best bet: Otega Oweh Under 21.5 Points (-120)

This pick does not mean I doubt Otega Oweh. I simply believe in the Missouri Tigers defense.

The best player on the Kentucky Wildcats’ roster deserves faith. He cleared this prop in Wednesday’s first round of the SEC Tournament despite going 0-for-3 from deep. Before that, he had cleared this prop in three straight and in five of six. In his last seven games, Oweh has averaged 23.3 points.

But expect the Tigers to slow this game down and to decisively cut into clean looks inside the arc, two aspects that work directly against Oweh’s scoring.

Considering Oweh has hit multiple 3-pointers in just eight of 19 SEC games, this may seem absurd, but in three of those, he still scored fewer than 22 points. The key in threading this needle against Missouri is that the Tigers give up plenty of looks from deep while not fouling at an aggressive rate.

Otega Oweh Over 1.5 made threes

Kentucky was a 12.5-point home favorite against Missouri on Jan. 7, losing outright by five. That may be a poor sign, but since then, the Wildcats rank No. 31 in barttorvik.com’s rankings, while the Tigers sit at No. 41.

Given the venue change, shortening this spread to -8 or -9 would have made sense, but hauling it down to -3.5 is an overreaction.

title: Giants fans get encouraging Cam Skattebo injury update
description: The New York Giants had something brewing last season with their rookie duo. Quarterback Jaxson Dart and running back Cam Skattebo turned into instant fan favorites, and their headbutt celebration kept going viral. The way they played was physical and…
fullText:
The New York Giants had something brewing last season with their rookie duo. Quarterback Jaxson Dart and running back Cam Skattebo turned into instant fan favorites, and their headbutt celebration kept going viral.

The way they played was physical and aggressive, the kind of football that gets fans in New York happy.

But that excitement for the Giants fans fell apart on Oct. 26 against the Philadelphia Eagles. Skattebo went down with a brutal injury that stopped the team’s momentum cold. The damage was severe, a dislocated ankle, an open tibia fracture, and a ruptured deltoid ligament.

MORE: Colts’ Daniel Jones sets timeline for return after new contract

Now, as the Giants try to build through free agency and the 2026 NFL Draft, focus is also on knowing when Skattebo will be back. On Wednesday, the running back gave fans something to feel good about. He joined ‘The Rich Eisen Show’ and talked about where he is with his injury recovery.

“I’m getting better. I ran for the first time today at about 75%, so it’s the bright side of things,” Skattebo said.

Eisen followed up, asking what running at 75% actually looks like and if it’s faster than a jog. Skattebo kept his humor.

“Not very fast, I’ll tell you that. I can get away from, like, a baby hippo, maybe. That’s about it. A little bit over a jog. Kind of just striding out, not exploding yet, and getting into that part of the game, but the jog and the striding.”

He made it clear the injury is the only thing slowing him down at this point, not the rehab itself. Skattebo said he has got about a month left before he is fully back, but he is confident in the timeline.

With OTAs coming up soon after the NFL draft, his goal is to wrap up the process the right way. By the time training camp rolls around in July, he expects to be ready to go.

Skattebo played eight games as a rookie and put up 410 yards with five touchdowns on 101 carries, averaging 4.1 yards per attempt.

title: Juventus have two different contract proposals for head coach Spalletti
description: Reports in Italy claim that talks over a new contract for Juventus head coach Luciano Spalletti are progressing and that the Bianconeri have drawn up two different proposals for the former CT to choos...
fullText:
Reports in Italy claim that talks over a new contract for Juventus head coach Luciano Spalletti are progressing and that the Bianconeri have drawn up two different proposals for the former CT to choose between.

Spalletti was appointed as the head coach of Juventus on October 30, signing a short-term contract until the end of the 2025-26 season following the dismissal of current Tottenham Hotspur boss Igor Tudor.

Since then, Spalletti has overseen a largely encouraging upturn in results, despite the club’s recent exit from the Champions League at the hands of Galatasaray. Juventus are currently fighting it out with Roma and Como for a spot in next season’s Champions League, but Spalletti’s future is not likely to be dependent on a top four finish according to recent reports.

According to Thursday’s updates from La Gazzetta dello Sport, talks over a contract extension for Spalletti are progressing, and as things stand, there are currently two options on the table for the head coach.

The first option would be for an initial 12-month contract until the end of the 2026-27 season with an option to renew for a further year. This would reportedly come with a salary offer of around €6m plus around €1m in bonuses.

The other option would be for a straight, two-year deal, albeit on slightly lower wages in the region of €5m per season. This option would effectively give Spalletti an extra year of job and salary security.

MILAN, ITALY – FEBRUARY 14: Luciano Spalletti, Head Coach of Juventus, looks on prior to the Serie A match between FC Internazionale and Juventus FC at Giuseppe Meazza Stadium on February 14, 2026 in Milan, Italy. (Photo by Marco Luzzani/Getty Images)

La Gazzetta dello Sport suggests that as things stand, the parties are leaning towards the first of these two options.

title: Cheltenham day three: Hold on to your hats
description: Windy conditions greet racegoers on St Patrick's Thursday at the world famous festival.
fullText:

The day has been branded "St Patrick's Thursday" with a strong Irish theme and the punters' focus on the much-anticipated Ryanair Chase and Paddy Power Stayers' Hurdle.
